sections.paragraph:
κορέννυμι 1 aor. ἐκορέσθην; pf. pass. ptc. κεκορεσμένος (Hom. et al.; Kaibel 314, 21f; ParJer; Jos., Ant. 2, 86; 10, 261) satiate, fill pass. be satiated, have enough w. gen. of that with which one is satiated or satisfied (Hom. et al.; SibOr 3, 697).

ⓐ lit. κορεσθέντες τροφῆς when they had eaten enough Ac 27:38.

ⓑ fig. (Kaibel, s. above; Appian, Hann. 27 §115, Bell. Civ. 1, 3, §10; Philo; Jos., Bell. 4, 314) ironically (Straub 24) ἤδη κεκορεσμένοι ἐστέ you already have eaten your fill, i.e., you think you already have all the spiritual food you need 1 Cor 4:8.—DELG s.v. κορε-; Frisk s.v. κορρένυμι. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

gen. gen. = genitive

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Straub Straub = WS., Die Bildersprache des Apostels Paulus—List 6

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 559
